author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tomāra īkṣaṇe haya, sakala utpatti laya,
caturdaśa bhuvanete jata
jaḍa jīva ādi kari’, tomāra kṛpāya hari,
labhe janma, āra ka’ba kata

tāhādera vṛtti jata, tomāra īkṣaṇe svataḥ,
janme, prabhu tumi sarveśvara
sakala jantura tumi, svābhāvika nityasvāmī,
suhṛnmitra⋅prāṇera īśvara

e bhaktivinoda kaya, śuna, prabhu dayāmaya,
bhaktaprati vātsalya tomāra
naisargika dharma haya, aupādhika kabhu naya,
dāse dayā hoiyā udāra
en_translation:
O Lord! By Your glance, all creation and dissolution occur in the fourteen planetary systems. Starting with inert matter and moving up to living beings, all obtain birth by Your mercy, O Hari. How much more can I say?

All their activities arise spontaneously by Your glance, O Lord, for You are the controller of all. You are the natural and eternal master of all living entities, a well-wishing friend, and the Lord of their lives.

Bhaktivinoda says: Listen, O merciful Lord! Your affection for Your devotees is a natural quality, never temporary or conditional. Please be generous with Your mercy to Your servant.
